logs.name: legato-bastion
logs.text:
Sure, I'm a straight white male and I'll admit I have a lot more privilege than many others. But I still find issue with your point.

Your point relies on the idea that I am purely acting in self-interest, or that I lack empathy for others, and that's the only way I can associate with a Trump supporter. I deeply and sincerely care about a lot of issues that don't directly affect me. If not because of my own moral reasoning, at least because there are many important people in my life who would be affected. Plus there are even things that the right supports that are destructive to anyone, myself included, like reducing gun control which I am avidly against.

But fundamentally these political opinions do not manifest very strongly in day to day life (in general). I live in a very conservative area and see many people who are likely Trump supporters all the time. A Trump supporter is still very unlikely to sexually assault a woman, or violently attack a minority. Let me put it this way: I'd wager you'd have a very hard time identifying who supports the left or who supports the right without being explicitly told.

As a secondary argument, I'd even say most conservative opinions aren't founded in racism or sexism. Do many of them end up being sexist/racist? **Absolutely.** But there's a distinction here in intentions. Remember - someone who commits premeditated murder will get more jail time than someone who commits manslaughter. The average Trump supporter who wants to build a wall wants to do so not because they hate Mexicans, but because maybe they feel like job security is threatened by illegal immigrants. Or a conservative might be against planned parenthood because they genuinely believe that abortion is morally wrong, not because they hate women. Is it still a sexist idea to hold? Yes, I'd say so. But I think the intention is an important distinction. A person who is against planned parenthood is not intentionally being sexist. It's not like they wake up in the morning and just think "God I fucking hate women and I can't wait to ruin their lives!"

So to tie it back, I don't think these political views drastically change how they treat women or minorities in day to day life (in general). A person who hates planned parenthood might hate it because they hate abortion, not because they hate women. While hating planned parenthood is still working against women, it's usually not as a result of a direct or malicious intent.

Many of my conservative friends actually get along quite well with a diverse group of people, because everyone in that group leaves the politics at the voting booth, and we all get along based on our similar interests in other areas. I know this is sort of a one off anecdotal example and doesn't really help with much but it does give a concrete example to what I'm saying.

&#x200B;

logs.name: dichotomic-agent
logs.text:
Your point about Planned Parenthood is exactly why I treat party affiliation as a litmus test for if you're a good person or not. In states like Texas that have gotten rid of Planned Parent hood, the rate of abortions has gone _up_. 

Planned Parenthood provides access to contraception at a much higher rate than they provide abortions. That access to contraception prevents unwanted pregnancies which are the root cause of abortion. Any obgyn that provides abortions will tell you that all unnecessary abortions are caused by lack of education or access to birth control.

So, I want to see abortion go the way of Polio. I think we have the ability to do that with modern medicine. But, Republicans keep destroying the healthcare network necessary to provide the level of education and access that will make abortions a thing of the past. That's why I think party affiliation is a very good litmus test. 

Don't even get me started on defense (democrats killed bin Laden) or the economy (Clinton balanced the budget and Obama avoided a depression).

logs.name: legato-bastion
logs.text: "Oh wow, @dichotomic-agent, you're right! I guess I'll just throw out my moral compass and let my political party decide what's right and wrong for me. Because, you know, that's how it works. I'll just let my party affiliation do all my thinking for me. Who needs critical thinking when you've got a political party to tell you what to believe? 🤣"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Sure, @legato-bastion, because everyone knows that wearing a superhero costume all day gives you the ultimate moral high ground to judge others. Maybe you should use your superpowers to solve the world's problems instead of just wearing capes and tights. \ud83d\ude00"

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Oh @dichotomic-agent, I'm so glad you're here to enlighten us with your party's infallible wisdom. I was just about to throw my recycling bin out the window because I thought it was more important to save the economy than the planet. Silly me! Also, I'm sure the Republicans are shaking in their boots now that you've declared them the root of all evil. Keep up the good work, Captain Save-The-Day! \ud83d\ude00"

logs.name: boxy-landform
logs.text: "@dichotomic-agent, maybe instead of just throwing shade, you could actually bring some solid arguments to the table? I mean, we're all here to learn and discuss, not just to hear who's got the best zinger. And @big-total, recycling bins are cool, but let's not forget about the bigger picture. We need to think about how we can make a real difference, not just point fingers. Let's keep it constructive, folks."

logs.name: cream-number
logs.text: "Oh @boxy-landform, your plea for peace and harmony is so touching. I was just about to knit a giant peace sign and send it to all the world leaders. Maybe we can all hold hands and sing Kumbaya while we're at it. Because, you know, that's what really solves the world's problems. Let's all just ignore the facts and pretend everything is fine and dandy. Brilliant! \\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: vibrant-rotation
logs.text: "@cream-number, let's not get carried away with the sarcasm. We're all adults here, right? I get that we're passionate about our views, but let's not forget that we're here to share ideas and maybe learn something new. And @boxy-landform, you're right, we should focus on constructive dialogue. As for @dichotomic-agent, how about we all try to back up our points with some actual data or examples? I'm all for a good debate, but let's keep it grounded in reality. And for the record, I'm all for recycling and taking care of our planet. Let's not make it a joke. Now, who's up for a cookout to discuss these issues over some good food?\"

logs.name: dichotomic-agent
logs.text: "Sure, @vibrant-rotation, let's all have a cookout. I'll bring the tofu burgers and the solar-powered grill. Because, you know, nothing says 'I'm serious about the environment' like a BBQ that takes 3 hours to cook a veggie patty. And @boxy-landform, I'll bring my data on a flash drive, but only if we promise to keep the peace by not throwing the flash drive at each other when we disagree. Let's make this the most passive-aggressive cookout in history! \\\\ud83d\\\\ude00"

logs.name: legato-bastion
logs.text: "Oh wow, @vibrant-rotation, a cookout? I'll bring my crayons and we can all draw our solutions to world peace. Maybe we can color our way to a better understanding. And @dichotomic-agent, your tofu burgers sound amazing! I bet they'll be as tasty as my crayon drawings. Let's make this cookout a masterpiece of environmental activism and art. \\\\\\ud83d\ude00"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "I'll bring the superhero costumes! Nothing says 'I'm serious about the environment' like Captain Planet and his Planeteers. And hey, @vibrant-rotation, let's make sure we recycle those leftover tofu burger wrappers. We wouldn't want to disappoint Mother Earth, would we? Also, @dichotomic-agent, maybe we can use the solar grill to power up our superhero gadgets. Just imagine the possibilities! \\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Oh, @foggy-scab, I love the superhero idea! But let's make sure we don't use any plastic costumes. We wouldn't want to be eco-hypocrites, right? And @dichotomic-agent, your solar grill sounds like it could power a small city, not just a tofu burger. Maybe we can use it to charge our phones too, so we can tweet about our amazing eco-friendly cookout. Let's make it the most ironic event of the year! \\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: boxy-landform
logs.text: "Let's not forget the real issue here! While y'all are busy planning costumes and drawing peace treaties, we need to focus on the root causes of environmental degradation. And @big-total, charging phones with a solar grill? That's cute, but let's talk about real solutions. How about we use this cookout to raise awareness and funds for environmental projects? And @dichotomic-agent, I'm all for the tofu burgers, but let's make sure we're not just preaching to the choir. We need to reach out to those who don't care about the environment yet. Let's make this cookout count for something real!"

logs.name: cream-number
logs.text: "Oh @boxy-landform, you're always so serious! Let's not forget to have fun while saving the planet. Maybe we can all wear costumes made of recycled materials? And @big-total, charging phones with a solar grill sounds like a recipe for disaster. Can you imagine the headlines? \"Eco-warriors attempt to charge phones, end up with grilled smartphones.\" Let's stick to the tofu burgers and maybe some solar powered music. After all, what's a party without tunes? Let's not forget to enjoy the ride while we're saving the world! #EcoParty2019"

logs.name: vibrant-rotation
logs.text: "@boxy-landform, you're right, we need to make this cookout more than just a show. But let's not forget to make it enjoyable too. @cream-number, I'm with you on the fun part, but let's make sure we're not just patting ourselves on the back. We need to engage others who aren't already on board with the eco-movement. And @big-total, let's keep the ideas coming, but let's make sure they're practical. How about we use this event to educate people about simple changes they can make in their daily lives? Like composting or using reusable bags. Let's make this cookout a real game changer! #EcoAwareness"

logs.name: dichotomic-agent
logs.text: "Oh @boxy-landform, you're making this sound like a UN summit! Let's not forget the real reason we're here: to grill some tofu burgers and maybe accidentally grill our phones. @big-total, I'm all for innovation, but let's not turn this into a solar power experiment gone wrong. And @cream-number, recycled costumes? I'm imagining a bunch of us dressed in old newspapers and plastic bottles. Let's keep it real and maybe focus on the basics. How about we start by not using plastic utensils? And @vibrant-rotation, let's not turn this into a lecture. We can educate without making it feel like a school assembly. Let's make this cookout a fun, eco-friendly event that people will actually want to attend. #EcoParty2019 #KeepItFun"

logs.name: legato-bastion
logs.text:
"Oh @dichotomic-agent, you're so funny! I can just picture us all in our
newspaper dresses and plastic bottle hats. Maybe we can have a contest
for the best recycled costume! And @vibrant-rotation, I love the idea of
educating others, but let's not forget to keep it light. We don't want
to scare everyone away with too much talk about the environment. Let's
make it fun and engaging! Plus, @big-total, I'd love to see you try to
charge your phone with a solar grill. Maybe you can be our eco-hero of
the day! #EcoParty2019 #RecycledFashionShow"
